title: Data Lab home access during COVID-19 lockdown | Stats NZ
content:
## Data Lab home access during COVID-19 lockdown
11 August 2020, 4:00pm
Stats NZ’s Integrated Data products played a vital role in the government's response to the COVID-19 outbreak. The Integrated Data team worked hard at short notice to enable continued access to the Data Lab environment for government priority projects during the lockdown period.
Data is an important aspect of policy development in that it provides evidence-based insights across a wide range of areas, so enabling the right access to it during the COVID-19 pandemic was crucial for the government's decision-making process during this time.
Key projects that were classified as government-critical included the project undertaken by Shaun Hendy’s team at University of Auckland. That modelling work fed directly into the National Crisis Management Centre’s response. From the government, The Treasury Department had several critical projects looking at modelling the effects of different lengths of lockdown on the country e.g. schools (financially), the economy and businesses. MBIE analysed the effects of lockdown on workers and businesses. Visit Datalab projects during COVID-19 for a full list of projects that were accessing the Data Lab environment remotely.
Only a capped number of projects that were classified as being directly linked to the COVID-19 response, or critical to government operations, were considered for home access to the Data Lab environment and certain requirements had to be met for this to be approved. Managers from key agencies were contacted in order to identify key researchers and projects.
From there, home arrangements were audited with a list of questions that considered home security arrangements, working from home environment, and signed confidentiality and security documents. There were a small number of individuals who had requested home access but didn’t meet the requirements and were not granted access.
The process of enabling home access for these projects was done at a short turnaround to enable the temporary access. We worked with IT departments from the agencies to set it up, which leveraged existing Data Lab connections. As Level 1 came into effect, the temporary remote access was shut down as all physical Data Labs reopened. This has allowed all approved researchers to be back using the physical Data Labs with standard secure practices.
The Integrated Data team strive to maintain a balance between accessibility to and security of the data. While we’ve demonstrated that the possibility for this access to exist, and that there is a clear demand for it, we still need it to be safe. We will continue to explore options for remote access in time, provided we can do so safely – providing assurances to our suppliers, and to New Zealand, that we are not compromising our stewardship role.
